global_notes: ALL duration_ns values are from ncu hardware-counter replay — typically 2-5x longer than real execution. Use only for relative comparisons between operators.
Four operators are marked is_fused=true. Their kernels overlap with the prologue's 16-kernel span. total_attributed_ns=4,721,771 is NOT a sequential sum — do not interpret as wall time.
A100 SXM4: ridge point BF16 = 156 FLOP/byte. Post-BF16 cast, the GEMM kernels will likely become memory-bandwidth-bound, not compute-bound.
12 aten::mm operators all use ampere_sgemm_128x64_tn (FP32 SIMT). smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active=0.0 on every single one. This is a single-root-cause profile.
QKV projections appear as 3 separate mm ops per attention layer (op_ids 4/5/6 for layer 1, op_ids 26/27/28 for layer 2). QKV fusion is structurally feasible.

optimizations.name: BF16 Dtype Promotion
operators_affected: aten::mm (all 12 nodes — Q/K/V/output projections, both layers)
aten::_efficient_attention_forward (all 3 nodes — fmha_cutlassF_f32 → BF16 FA path)

bottleneck.description: All GEMM kernels are dispatched to the FP32 SIMT path (ampere_sgemm_128x64_tn). smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active=0.0 on every mm kernel — Tensor Cores are completely idle. On A100, FP32 SIMT peak is 19.5 TFLOPS vs 312 BF16 Tensor Core TFLOPS — a 16x gap. The SDPA kernels (fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80) compound this with 168 registers/thread, causing 4,714,496 bytes of local memory spills per invocation and occupancy of only ~15.9%.

transformation.description: Cast model weights and input tensor to torch.bfloat16 before torch.compile(). This is a non-graph change — memory dtype is not visible in the FX IR and cannot be applied as an FX pass. BF16 routes cuBLAS from ampere_sgemm_128x64_tn (SIMT) to sm80_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16_tn (HMMA Tensor Cores). It also switches the SDPA path from fmha_cutlassF_f32 (168 regs/thread, spills) to the BF16 FlashAttention variant (lower register pressure, higher occupancy).
transformation.code_hint: BF16 must be applied BEFORE torch.compile(). Dynamo traces at the dtype present at compile time. Applying dtype inside the graph or after compile has no effect on cuBLAS kernel selection.

sdpa_dispatch_change.after: fmha_cutlassF_bf16_aligned_*  or PyTorch 2.x FlashAttention BF16 path  (lower register count, no local spills)

estimated_impact.rationale: GEMMs currently saturate FP32 SIMT at 84% SM throughput. After BF16 cast, the same SM utilization operates 16x faster throughput (Tensor Core vs SIMT). Real-world speedup is bounded by memory bandwidth becoming the new bottleneck (A100 HBM = 2.0 TB/s, ridge point 156 FLOP/byte). Empirical range for transformer GEMM at this size (M=4096, N=K=512): 4-8x kernel speedup. SDPA benefits separately from elimination of register spills. Net wall-time reduction estimated 35-50% accounting for the non-GEMM operators being unchanged.
estimated_impact.new_bottleneck_after_opt: memory_bandwidth — GEMMs will be bandwidth-bound at BF16 sizes; consider QKV fusion (OPT-2) to raise arithmetic intensity

notes: Tied-embedding contraindication does not apply: SDPAAttentionBlock has no tied embedding + output-projection weight sharing.
torch.bfloat16 is preferred over float16 on A100: BF16 has the same dynamic range as FP32 (wider exponent), reducing the risk of overflow in attention logits without requiring loss scaling.
After this change, re-run the profiler and verify kernel name changes from ampere_sgemm to sm80_xmma_gemm in the ncu output.
Optionally add: torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True as a fallback if BF16 is not feasible, but this does not match the guaranteed cuBLAS Tensor Core routing that BF16 provides.

optimizations.name: QKV Weight Fusion (per attention layer)

bottleneck.description: Q, K, and V projections are dispatched as 3 independent GEMM kernels per attention layer. Each kernel operates on the same input activation (GEMM shape M=4096, N=K=512). Three separate kernel launches means 3x the kernel launch overhead and 3x the weight tensor reads from HBM. After BF16 conversion (OPT-1), these GEMMs will become bandwidth-bound; fusing them into a single [N=1536, K=512] GEMM raises arithmetic intensity and reduces HBM reads from 3×W to 1×W_fused.

evidence.note: Profiler shows 3 distinct mm ops sharing the same input for Q/K/V respectively. The separate op_ids confirm they are not already batched by Inductor.

transformation.description: Manual per-rep FX pass. Detect 3 F.linear nodes sharing the same input placeholder, concatenate their weight tensors into W_qkv = cat([W_q, W_k, W_v], dim=0), register it as a buffer, replace the 3 linear calls with 1 linear call followed by torch.chunk(3, dim=-1).
transformation.location: Custom backend registered via torch._dynamo.register_backend — applied in the pre-Inductor FX graph before compile_fx.
fx_steps: # Step 1: Capture actual weight tensor values from partition inputs
# (Dynamo lifts all nn.Module parameters as placeholder nodes)
# Step 2: Apply the QKV fusion pass per unique rep
# - Map placeholder node -> tensor: ph_to_tensor = {ph: t for ph, t in zip(placeholders, partition_inputs)}
# - Group F.linear calls by first argument (x input node name)
# - For groups of 3+: extract W_q, W_k, W_v from ph_to_tensor
# - Validate W_q.shape[1] == W_k.shape[1] == W_v.shape[1] (K dims must match)
# - W_qkv = torch.cat([W_q, W_k, W_v], dim=0)  # shape: [3*N_out, K]
# - Erase old q_lin, k_lin, v_lin nodes
transformation.why_not_replace_pattern: replace_pattern cannot access actual tensor values from placeholder nodes. Weight shapes must be validated and W_qkv must be materialized at pass time. This requires the manual per-rep approach with partition_inputs.
transformation.inductor_fusion_check: Verify Inductor has NOT already fused Q/K/V: the profile shows 3 separate aten::mm op_ids with distinct kernel invocations and distinct call_indices, confirming no Inductor-level batching occurred.

estimated_impact.rationale: QKV fusion reduces 3 GEMM kernel launches to 1 per layer (6 kernels -> 2 kernels across both layers). After BF16 (OPT-1), the fused [N=1536, K=512] GEMM has 3x higher arithmetic intensity than the individual [N=512, K=512] GEMMs, which matters once the workload becomes bandwidth-bound. Empirical range based on similar attention workloads at batch=1, seq=4096: 5-15% reduction in GEMM time, translating to ~3-8% total wall-time reduction given GEMM fraction.
estimated_impact.new_arithmetic_intensity: W_qkv is [1536, 512] vs 3x[512, 512] — same FLOPs, 1/3 the weight reads from HBM

optimizations.dependency_reason: QKV fusion must be applied after BF16 dtype promotion. The fused W_qkv buffer is registered at pass time with the current model dtype. If the pass runs before the BF16 cast, W_qkv is FP32; the subsequent BF16 cast does not retroactively recast registered buffers unless explicitly iterated. Fusion at BF16 avoids a mixed-dtype mm call.
notes: Confidence is medium rather than high because: (1) the profiler attribute names show the Q/K/V mm calls but do not confirm they share the exact same input node in the pre-Inductor FX graph — this must be verified by inspecting the graph at backend registration time; (2) if biases differ in shape or any projection has bias=False while others have bias=True, the grouped detection may partially match.

If the 3 projections have different output sizes (e.g., multi-query attention with N_k != N_q), the shape validation guard (W_q.shape[1] == W_k.shape[1] == W_v.shape[1]) will reject the fusion gracefully.
The pass must be applied per unique layer rep and propagated to duplicate reps — see _capture_partition_inputs utility in fx-patterns.md.
Inductor may independently re-fuse the chunk outputs back together; this is expected behavior and not a conflict.

optimizations.name: SDPA FlashAttention Replacement

bottleneck.description: All 3 SDPA forward calls dispatch to fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80 — the FP32 CUTLASS xformers kernel. This kernel uses 168 registers/thread (exceeding the 128-register warp-full-occupancy threshold), causing 4,714,496 bytes of local memory spills per invocation to L2/HBM. The resulting achieved_occupancy of ~15.9% is below the 20% wave_starvation threshold. SM utilization is 45.5% despite Tensor Cores being partially active at 39.6% — the gap is explained by the memory latency stalls from local spills.

transformation.description: Replace the manual attention pattern (q@k.T * scale → softmax → @v) with F.scaled_dot_product_attention. After BF16 (OPT-1), this dispatches to the PyTorch 2.x FlashAttention BF16 path, which uses a fused Triton or cuDNN kernel with significantly lower register pressure and no local memory spills. NOTE: if the model already uses F.scaled_dot_product_attention or xformers memory_efficient_attention at the Python level, Dynamo will trace these as aten::_scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention — in that case this FX pass is not needed; the BF16 cast alone (OPT-1) will trigger the BF16 dispatch path.
transformation.location: Custom backend FX pass — applied in the pre-Inductor graph.
fx_steps: # Anchor: find call_function(operator.matmul) nodes (the final attn @ v multiply)
if n.op != 'call_function' or n.target is not operator.matmul: continue
# Verify attn is output of torch.softmax
if not (attn_node.op == 'call_function' and attn_node.target is torch.softmax): continue
# Verify softmax input is scaled scores: scores * scale (call_function operator.mul)
if not (scaled_node.op == 'call_function' and scaled_node.target is operator.mul): continue
# Verify scores is q @ k_t (call_function operator.matmul)
if not (qk_node.op == 'call_function' and qk_node.target is operator.matmul): continue
# Unwrap k.transpose(-2,-1) — F.scaled_dot_product_attention transposes k internally
continue  # cannot recover bare k — skip
# Erase dead nodes: n, attn_node, scaled_node, qk_node (if no other users)
for dead in (n, attn_node, scaled_node, qk_node):
transformation.why_replace_pattern_fails: Inductor decomposes torch.softmax into exp+sum+div before the FX pass sees the graph. The replace_pattern utility matches graph structure exactly and cannot handle this decomposition. Manual traversal anchored on the final operator.matmul is required.
transformation.context_check: The triage shows aten::_efficient_attention_forward and aten::_scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention in fused_with fields. This suggests the model may already be using F.scaled_dot_product_attention at the Python level and Dynamo has traced it to the xformers efficient attention path. In that case: (a) this FX pass is unnecessary — OPT-1 alone fixes the dispatch, and (b) if the fmha_cutlassF_f32 kernel still appears after OPT-1, the fallback is to explicitly force the flash_sdp backend via torch.backends.cuda.sdp_kernel(enable_flash=True, enable_math=False).

estimated_impact.latency_reduction_pct: 30-50 on SDPA ops (translates to 5-9% total wall-time)
estimated_impact.rationale: Eliminating 168-register spills and raising occupancy from ~16% to ~50%+ (typical for FlashAttention BF16 on A100) directly reduces the SDPA kernel duration. The 3 SDPA ops account for 17.35% of attributed time; a 30-50% improvement on those ops yields ~5-9% total reduction. Combined with OPT-1 (which already partially improves SDPA via dtype change), the incremental gain of this FX pass over OPT-1 alone is 5-10% total if the model is not already using F.sdpa.

optimizations.dependency_reason: F.scaled_dot_product_attention requires uniform dtype across Q, K, V. Apply after BF16 cast (OPT-1) to ensure the inserted SDPA node operates on BF16 inputs and dispatches to the FlashAttention BF16 kernel rather than the same fmha_cutlassF_f32 path.
notes: Confidence is medium: the triage shows aten::_scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention in the fused_with field, which implies the model may already emit F.scaled_dot_product_attention at the Python level. If so, this pass is a no-op and OPT-1 alone handles the dispatch improvement.
After OPT-1: re-run the profiler. If the kernel name changes from fmha_cutlassF_f32 to fmha_cutlassF_bf16 or to a Triton FlashAttention kernel, the SDPA replacement FX pass is not needed.

If F.scaled_dot_product_attention still dispatches to fmha_cutlassF after OPT-1, explicitly enable Flash attention: torch.backends.cuda.sdp_kernel(enable_flash=True, enable_math=False, enable_mem_efficient=False).
is_causal=False is safe here (default) — do not pass is_causal=True unless the model has a causal mask, as it changes the mathematical semantics.

The k_t_node unwrapping step (extracting bare k from k.transpose(-2,-1)) is critical — F.scaled_dot_product_attention transposes K internally and passing an already-transposed K would compute attention incorrectly.

optimizations.name: max-autotune Compile Mode

bottleneck.description: After OPT-1 (BF16), the GEMMs will transition from SIMT-bound to potentially bandwidth-bound. The cuBLAS heuristic selected at compile time may not pick the highest-throughput tile configuration for the specific shapes in this model (M=4096, N=512/1536, K=512). Autotune exhaustively benchmarks tile sizes and algorithm variants to find the optimal kernel for each unique GEMM shape.

evidence.note: SM throughput of 84.5% on FP32 SIMT does not imply the same utilization will transfer to Tensor Cores — the tile size and warp scheduling strategy differs between SIMT and TC kernels. Autotune closes this gap.

transformation.description: Change torch.compile mode from 'default' to 'max-autotune'. No graph changes required.

estimated_impact.rationale: max-autotune is most impactful for non-square GEMMs (M=4096, N=512 is highly non-square). The cuBLAS default heuristic is optimized for square matrices. Autotune can find split-K or other algorithmic variants that recover 5-15% on non-square shapes.
estimated_impact.compile_time_cost_seconds: 30-120 (first run only, then cached in torch_compile_debug)

notes: max-autotune has a significant first-run compile overhead (typically 1-3 minutes for a 2-layer transformer). Cache the compiled artifact with torch.export or rely on the default Inductor cache directory.
Combine with TORCH_COMPILE_CACHE_DIR env var to persist the autotune cache across runs.
This optimization is independent of OPT-2 and OPT-3 and can be applied alongside them.

application_order.description: Dependency DAG — apply in this exact sequence to avoid dtype/buffer mismatches.

ordered_steps.when: In custom backend FX pass, after OPT-1 has set dtype to BF16

ordered_steps.action: _pass_replace_sdpa(rep_mod) — only if re-profiling after OPT-1 still shows fmha_cutlassF_f32
ordered_steps.when: In custom backend FX pass, after OPT-1. Verify necessity by checking kernel name post-OPT-1.
Replace mode='default' with mode='max-autotune' at the compile call site, after OPT-1

dag_edges.reason: QKV fused buffer must be registered at BF16 dtype
F.sdpa requires uniform dtype; FlashAttention BF16 path requires BF16 inputs
max-autotune searches among Tensor Core kernels — meaningful only after BF16 cast activates TC dispatch

optimizations_not_applicable.reason: SDPAAttentionBlock contains no Conv2d operators. channels_last is only relevant for NHWC convolution. No convertTensor_kernel detected in profile.
No F.batch_norm nodes in the model. Not applicable.
No aten::tanh or torch.tanh nodes detected in the profile kernels. The FFN activations in this model do not use tanh.

The ampere_sgemm_128x64_tn kernel already handles transposed weight access natively (_tn suffix = transpose-N layout). After BF16 cast (OPT-1), the sm80_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16_tn kernel also handles this. Pre-transposing weights would eliminate the _tn suffix but the gain is marginal relative to the Tensor Core activation gap. Not worth the doubled weight memory cost at this shape.
